text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I'm taking a walk through the park one day when
the sound of a familiar voice brings me to a stop.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Yeeesss! Thank heavens! Could you come over
here, Trainer?

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text: Boy, am I glad that you happened by.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
The thing is, I need you to be the usher in this
wedding that's about to start.

text_block_list.name: Girl
text_block_list.text:
Is this the usher? Great! Then, let's hurry and
start!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I look in the direction of the voice to find a boy
and girl around preschool age sitting happily on a
picnic sheet.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
So basically... these kids are having a pretend
wedding and I got roped into playing the
officiant.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
But they started raising a fuss about there being
no usher. So... hehe... would you mind helping
out?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I decide to take on the role of usher to assist a
troubled Matikanetannhauser.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Now then, bride and groom. Do you both vow to
love each other in sickness and in health?

text_block_list.name: Bride & Groom
text_block_list.text: I do!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Well said! Then, I now pronounce you husband
and wife!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
...Let's see, what comes next again? Uhhh...
Hmm...

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Well, I guess I'll just make up some vows of my
own.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Anytime, anywhere, in sickness and in health, I
vow to make you vow every vow ever vowed.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
And you, usher, do you vow to make our bride
and groom feel comfortable anytime and
anywhere, no matter what?

text_block_list.name: Groom
text_block_list.text:
Um... excuse me. Doesn't this wedding have too
many vows?

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Wh-wh-what are you saying?! This ceremony is
being run perfectly by-the-book! Now, next is...
Um...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Despite carefully faking her way through the
wedding thus far, Matikanetannhauser finally
starts to stumble. That's when...

text_block_list.name: Mysterious Girl
text_block_list.text:
Wait just a minute! Just what do you think you're
doing getting married?!

text_block_list.name: Groom
text_block_list.text:
Agh! W-wait, it's not what it looks like! We're just
playing!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text: Ummm... What's going on?

text_block_list.name: Mysterious Girl
text_block_list.text:
Just playing, huh? So this is all just a game to
you? Including me? I bet it made you feel like a
big boy, toying with a maiden's heart!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I don't quite grasp the situation—nor do I want
to—but if I didn't know better, I'd say this is a
lovers' quarrel!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Mun-mun... We can't let a fight break out! As the
grown-ups here, we have to put a stop to this!
Let's go, Trainer!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Hey! HEY! Everyone, listen up! You all know who
this is, right?

text_block_list.name: Bride
text_block_list.text: Isn't that the usher?

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Exactly! Ushers are here to escort us all to where
we belong! You must never let them see you
fighting, or they'll escort you right outside!

text_block_list.name: Mysterious Girl
text_block_list.text:
What are you talking about?! We're already
outside!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text: New plan! Quick, Trainer! Fire your Usher Beam!

text_block_list.name: Children
text_block_list.text: Hyaaah!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
There. Now you've all become the best of friends.
I want the three of you to play together nicely
now, you hear?

text_block_list.name: Children
text_block_list.text: Y-yes, ma'am!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Matikanetannhauser uses her quick wits—or
something like that—to put a stop to the fight
before things get out of hand.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
As officiant, I've vowed to make everyone else
swear vows.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Hehehe! Then, by the power invested in me as
officiant, I lay down my benevolent decree.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
All of you need to deepen your understanding of
love so you don't get into any more fights.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Which meeeeans... it's time for Officiant
Machitan's Love Seminar!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
So, let's see here... lesson one! Don't go around
telling just anybody that you love them! Also...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Although it seems more like a seminar in common
sense than love, Matikanetannhauser successfully
delivers a sermon on morality.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Heh-heh! I've always been good with children.
Whenever my little brother got into fights with his
friends, I was the one who had to break things up.

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
Now, we could continue the ceremony... but
things will probably just get messy again, so let's
play something else!

text_block_list.name: Matikanetannhauser
text_block_list.text:
How about tag? Red Light, Green Light? Just
promise that you'll all play nice!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The children appear to have settled their
differences, so all five of us play together happily
for a while.
